name: pytdc
tagline: Wraps the PyTDC Python library for loading drug-discovery ML datasets, benchmarks, splits, and molecular generation oracles.
verdict: No security risks found: no credential access, no piped/remote code execution, no hidden or manipulative instructions, and the install command (uv pip install PyTDC) matches the real, well-known PyPI/GitHub package from Harvard's TDC project. Minor quality gaps only: no explicit 'when NOT to use' guidance and no documented failure-path handling (bad dataset

highlights.summary: No section states when NOT to use the skill (e.g., non-drug ML tasks, production inference vs. research benchmarking); a user could misapply it outside its intended research/benchmarking scope with no
Individual sections describe return shapes informally ('Returns dict with train/valid/test DataFrames', line 66; column names, lines 138-141) but there is no single explicit output-contract statement
No guidance anywhere for handling errors: invalid dataset/oracle names, network failures during dataset download, or missing optional dependencies auto-installed 'as needed' (line 42).
